sections.heading: Production Operations Commands
sections.prose: Operational commands for health, logs, incident response, and rollback.

sections.heading: Service Health Targets
sections.prose:
- `contact360.io/api`: `/health`, `/health/db`
- `contact360.io/admin`: Django app health endpoint or service status
- `contact360.io/app`, `contact360.io/root`, `contact360.io/email`: HTTP root and key route checks
- `contact360.io/sync`, `contact360.io/jobs`, `lambda/*`, `backend(dev)/*`: service health route and logs

items.text: `contact360.io/api`: `/health`, `/health/db`
`contact360.io/admin`: Django app health endpoint or service status
`contact360.io/app`, `contact360.io/root`, `contact360.io/email`: HTTP root and key route checks
`contact360.io/sync`, `contact360.io/jobs`, `lambda/*`, `backend(dev)/*`: service health route and logs

sections.heading: PM2 Operations (if applicable)
sections.prose:
```bash
pm2 list
pm2 logs <process-name>
pm2 restart <process-name>
```

code_blocks.value:
pm2 list
pm2 logs <process-name>
pm2 restart <process-name>

sections.heading: System Services (if applicable)
sections.prose:
```bash
sudo systemctl status <service-name>
sudo systemctl restart <service-name>
```

code_blocks.value:
sudo systemctl status <service-name>
sudo systemctl restart <service-name>

sections.heading: Frontend Rollback Pattern
sections.prose:
```bash
git checkout <known-good-tag>
npm ci
npm run build
pm2 restart <process-name>
```

code_blocks.value:
git checkout <known-good-tag>
npm ci
npm run build
pm2 restart <process-name>

sections.heading: redeploy known-good artifact or stack version
Incident First Commands
sections.prose:
```bash
date
git rev-parse HEAD
curl -I https://<HOST_OR_DOMAIN>
```

code_blocks.value:
date
git rev-parse HEAD
curl -I https://<HOST_OR_DOMAIN>

sections.heading: Operational Guardrails
sections.prose:
- Do not run destructive database commands in production from docs defaults.
- Always capture request IDs / trace IDs when triaging.
- Keep rollback references aligned with version tags from `docs/versions.md`.

items.text: Do not run destructive database commands in production from docs defaults.
Always capture request IDs / trace IDs when triaging.
Keep rollback references aligned with version tags from `docs/versions.md`.

sections.heading: Small Task Breakdown
sections.prose:
1. Confirm incident scope (service and environment).
2. Collect health and logs.
3. Identify last known good tag/commit.
4. Roll forward or rollback based on impact.
5. Verify health and close with evidence.

items.text: Confirm incident scope (service and environment).
Collect health and logs.
Identify last known good tag/commit.
Roll forward or rollback based on impact.
Verify health and close with evidence.

sections.heading: I) Detection
sections.prose:
- Confirm incident with health checks and user-facing signal.
- Identify impacted services and blast radius.

items.text: Confirm incident with health checks and user-facing signal.
Identify impacted services and blast radius.

sections.heading: II) Triage
sections.prose:
- Gather logs and request/trace IDs.
- Correlate with deploy history and config changes.

items.text: Gather logs and request/trace IDs.
Correlate with deploy history and config changes.

sections.heading: III) Mitigation
sections.prose:
- Apply immediate mitigation (restart, rollback, traffic control).
- Validate partial recovery before full closure.

items.text: Apply immediate mitigation (restart, rollback, traffic control).
Validate partial recovery before full closure.

sections.heading: IV) Recovery Validation
sections.prose:
- Run health and smoke checks.
- Verify no regression in connected services.

items.text: Run health and smoke checks.
Verify no regression in connected services.

sections.heading: V) Closure
sections.prose: - Document timeline, root cause summary, mitigation command list, and follow-up tasks.

items.text: Document timeline, root cause summary, mitigation command list, and follow-up tasks.
